task_completion.description: The agent switches the medical viewer to the Sagittal view to visualize the neural foramina.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main viewport of the medical imaging software shows a sagittal slice (side view of the spine) where the neural foramina (keyhole shapes between vertebrae) are visible.

task_completion.description: The agent performs measurements on the L4-L5 neural foramen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Measurement lines (rulers) are visible spanning the vertical (height) and/or horizontal (width) dimensions of the foramen between the L4 and L5 vertebrae in the sagittal view.

task_completion.description: The agent performs measurements on the L5-S1 neural foramen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Measurement lines (rulers) are visible spanning the vertical (height) and/or horizontal (width) dimensions of the foramen between the L5 vertebra and the Sacrum (S1) in the sagittal view.

task_completion.description: The agent verifies the collected measurements in the software's markup or measurement module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A panel, table, or list is visible in the software interface showing multiple measurement entries (likely in mm), indicating that height and width were recorded for multiple levels/sides.

task_completion.description: The agent generates a JSON report containing the required metrics (height, width, area, grade).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal, or preview window displays a JSON structure with keys such as 'L4L5_left', 'grade', 'area_mm2', and 'clinical_impression'.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the measurement markups and the assessment report to the specified paths.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog, file explorer, or terminal command confirms the saving of 'foramen_measurements.mrk.json' and 'foramen_report.json' to the '~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent uses the GUI tools to manually place measurement points on the scan.
integrity.visual_evidence: The mouse cursor is visible interacting with the image viewer to draw lines or place points, rather than measurements appearing instantly via script.

integrity.description: The agent performs the task within a dedicated medical image viewer (e.g., 3D Slicer).
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the interface of a medical imaging application with DICOM viewing capabilities, not just a code editor or generic image viewer.

integrity.description: The values in the final report appear consistent with the visual measurements shown in the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numbers visible in the measurement panel (e.g., ~12mm) roughly match the values entered into the final JSON report (e.g., Grade 1 or ~12mm).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The standard critical height for the lumbar neural foramen is 15 mm. A foraminal height of 15 mm or less is significantly associated with nerve root compression. Grading criteria: Grade 0 (Normal) >= 15mm; Grade 1 (Mild) 10-15mm; Grade 2 (Moderate) 5-10mm; Grade 3 (Severe) < 5mm.
